summary: P0-Task 1 (data-driven content + admin Website Text/Media pages) verified end-to-end. All 5 new backend pytest cases pass against the public URL. Frontend flows pass: public home renders identically with seed content, admin login (direct success now that OTP/MFA is off), Website Text edit reflects on home page (manifesto + nav.1.label both reverted cleanly), and Website Media slot upload appears on the public home (first pillar img becomes a data: URL src) with a working AlertDialog (Keep it / Yes, remove it) to remove.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminShell.jsx builds nav data-testids by lowercasing label and replacing spaces with '-'. For 'Website Images & Videos' the '&' is preserved -> 'admin-nav-website-images-&-videos', which is unusual and made one of my queries return 0 hits even though the link rendered. Consider sanitising the label to a route-safe slug (also strip '&'/non-alphanum) for cleaner test ids.

WebsiteText.jsx 'Saved' indicator only renders for ~2.2s and the save() callback awaits load()+refresh() before flipping `saved=true`. In practice the flag is briefly true after content reloads, but Playwright wait_for_selector(saved-X) was timing out intermittently in this run even though the change persisted on the public site. Consider showing the saved state earlier (immediately after the PUT resolves, before the secondary reload) or extending the visible window.
Idempotent content seed in server.py looks correct (uses $setOnInsert keyed by 'key'). 124 keys served by /api/content as expected.
Public /api/content correctly excludes _id (projection {_id:0,key:1,value:1}) and returns a clean flat map.

PUT /api/admin/content uses update_one without upsert; if a key is missing in the collection the update silently no-ops. Since admin can only edit keys already returned from GET /admin/content this is fine today, but worth upgrading to upsert=True for forward compatibility when new DEFAULT_CONTENT entries land before a redeploy.
Bearer auth on /admin/content endpoints validated (401 without token).

success_rate.backend: 100% (5/5 new content tests, public + admin + media round-trip)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical flows (home render, admin login, text edit + reflect + revert, nav label edit + reflect + revert, media upload + reflect + remove dialog + cleanup)

test_credentials: info@oncewerewild.com / WildAtHeart2026. OTP/MFA disabled (OTP_LOGIN_ENABLED=false), so /api/auth/login returns {status:'success', access_token, user} directly.
seed_data_creation: Each test created TEST_-prefixed records and cleaned them up. Content keys that were edited (home.manifesto.heading, nav.1.label) were reverted to seed values. Public /api/content reflects original seed values at end of run (124 keys).
context_for_next_testing_agent: OTP/MFA is disabled by env flag, so login is single-step. Public content map should always contain 124 keys after seed; if you add more DEFAULT_CONTENT entries, bump the threshold in test_content.py. Admin nav data-testid for the media link is unusual: 'admin-nav-website-images-&-videos' (keeps the &). Prefer querying by visible text or by route when checking sidebar links. The 'saved-{group}' indicator is short-lived; assert via the persisted public value instead of waiting on the indicator.
rca of the issue: No bugs identified in this iteration.
